title: Incognito: The Secret Lives of the Brain
author: David Eagleman
themes: The Brain as a Team of Rivals
The Neuroscience of Blame and Responsibility
excerpt: Most of what your brain does happens beneath the radar of your conscious awareness. David Eagleman takes us on a mind-bending tour of the hidden subroutines that drive our behavior, revealing that the conscious mind is just the tip of an enormous neural iceberg.
keyIdeas: The conscious mind is just the tip of the iceberg—most brain activity is unconscious
The brain is a collection of competing neural networks, not a single unified self
Perception is a controlled hallucination—the brain constructs reality from incomplete data
Our behavior is influenced by factors we are completely unaware of
keyTakeaways: You have less free will than you think—much of your behavior is determined by unconscious processes
The feeling of conscious choice is often an after-the-fact narrative
Understanding the unconscious brain leads to greater self-compassion
The brain's parallel processing architecture explains many cognitive mysteries
whoShouldRead: Anyone curious about consciousness and the nature of the self
Readers interested in the philosophical implications of brain science
Fans of Oliver Sacks and Oliver Sacks-style case studies
whoShouldSkip: Those uncomfortable with challenges to free will and personal responsibility
Readers looking for practical self-help rather than mind-expanding science
